body.title: Consequence check: anticipatory brief + token tracking
body.result: CLEAR: pre-brief surfaces decision siblings and file-proximity; output is concise; no runaway cost observed; token log shows 0 overhead when no context exists
body.intention: I intend that this ticket verifies token tracking shows measurable reduction from anticipatory memory and that the pre-brief doesn't itself become a token sink.
body.description:
**What changed:** sprint-ticket has a proactive pre-brief step; token consumption is now tracked per sprint.
**Predicted unintended effects:** The pre-brief itself costs tokens — if poorly bounded it could add more than it saves. Librarian briefing assembly could be slow, adding latency to sprint start. Token log could grow unbounded.
**Signals to watch:** Compare sprint_tokens.log before and after T-sprint-anticipatory-brief ships. Does per-sprint token spend decrease? Does the pre-brief step itself stay under its 500-token cap? Sprint latency at start.
**Gate condition:** Check after 5+ sprints post-ship.

**Worst-case token spend:**
  - Per-sprint briefing cost: ~500 tokens (capped in T-sprint-anticipatory-brief)
  - Hard cap: 500 tokens enforced in skill step
  - Blast radius: briefing assembly that loops or over-fetches; Librarian recall has no retry loop so blast radius is one over-budget call per sprint

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Read sprint_tokens.log; compare pre/post; annotate outcome and close.
**Completion criteria:** Outcome annotated (occurred / clear / partial) and ticket closed.
